operating_state: Total revenue increased 5% to $85.8 billion in Q3 2024, driven by Services growth of 14% and iPad growth of 24%, while iPhone remained flat year-over-year. Regional performance showed broad strength with Americas, Europe, and Rest of Asia Pacific posting mid-to-high single digit growth, offset by Greater China declining 7% primarily due to lower iPhone sales. Nine-month revenue growth moderated to 1% at $296.1 billion as iPhone and iPad declines offset Services momentum.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 46.3% from 44.5% in the prior year quarter, with Services margin improving to 74.0% from 70.5% due to mix changes while Products margin held steady at 35.3%. Operating expenses increased to 17% of revenue from 16% in the prior year, driven by research and development headcount expansion and modest selling expense growth. The effective tax rate increased to 15.9% from 12.5% due to higher foreign tax rates and reduced share-based compensation benefits.
material_events: The company announced new iPad Air and iPad Pro models along with Apple Intelligence, a generative AI system integrated across iOS 18, macOS Sequoia, and iPadOS 18. Share repurchases totaled $26.0 billion during the quarter with $3.9 billion in dividends paid, maintaining the quarterly dividend at $0.25 per share. A preliminary settlement was approved for consolidated derivative litigation cases.
macro_exposures: Currency headwinds impacted multiple regions with renminbi weakness affecting Greater China results and yen weakness pressuring Japan performance. Manufacturing purchase obligations increased to $38.4 billion reflecting supply chain commitments amid ongoing macroeconomic volatility including inflation and interest rate pressures.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued annual dividend increases subject to board approval and maintains confidence in cash generation supporting capital return programs. The company anticipates gross margin volatility while investing in research and development capabilities. Apple Intelligence rollout across device ecosystem represents a strategic AI integration initiative.
uncertainty_profile: New accounting standards will require enhanced disclosures beginning in Q4 2026 and expanded segment reporting starting Q4 2025. The company faces ongoing risks from macroeconomic conditions, currency fluctuations, competitive pressures, manufacturing dependencies, and supply chain obligations that could materially impact future results.
